description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: CAT-005 proposal: Analyst proposes new section "V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything" covering 12+ affected WINs. Cascade failure analysis shows refraction needed is 13-27 degrees vs 0.03 degrees real. Human must decide: approve as new section (Part 2 or Section 7 extension), reject, or integrate as individual WIN patches.
issues.human_decision: Approved as new section under The Model tab (option a). New code_analysis sub-category tag requested for cascade-affected WINs. Analyst to write recommended proposal package — route to curmudgeon before fleshing out prose. See CAT-005.json human_decision block.
issues.gate_returned_reason: Curmudgeon gate (2026-04-10): ACCEPT_WITH_MODIFICATIONS — 7 blockers. Analyst must produce EXP-050-numerics.json, re-audit WIN flip table (per-WIN re-read required), withdraw cascade_dependent tag, use structural title, renumber to Section 1.8. Resubmit for second gate pass.
issues.gate2_conditions: WIN-018 flip rejected; WIN-044+WIN-068 flips approved; 8-row table; 400 words net-new; physical-vs-apparent distinction explicit

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.title: SEC-4.5.9 false claim: d_geo is NOT undefined — the dome defines it as spherical distance
issues.description: Section 4.5.9 states d_geo is undefined and never specified. This is false — the dome README defines d_geo as spherical arc distance. The actual critique should be that (a) claiming flat geometry while using spherical-distance inputs is self-contradictory, and (b) the model uses MDS with geodesic distances as anchors then claims the resulting layout proves flat geometry — circular reasoning. Review: SEC-4.5.9.json.

issues.title: predictions-catalog: build schema mismatch — PRED_STD_RELABEL uses derivation field that does not exist
issues.description: generate-html.js counts stdRelabel via entries.filter(e => e.derivation === "standard_physics"), but no entry has a derivation field. Same for testability, test_window.status. These four build placeholders (PRED_STD_RELABEL, PRED_TESTABLE, PRED_DOME_DERIVED, PRED_ACTIVE_WINDOWS) are permanently 0. Not currently rendered in section HTML but noted in _meta as ISS-730. May conflict with ISS-730 — check.

issues.title: predictions-catalog: no documented rule for restates_win + non-recycled verdict combinations
issues.description: 12 entries have restates_win set (pointing to WIN) but their verdict is NOT recycled (standard_physics/unfalsifiable/pending). No documented rule explains when restating a WIN warrants recycled vs a substantive verdict. Creates an attack surface: dome defender can say we are double-counting by calling the same domain both unfalsifiable and recycled. Need _meta taxonomy note.

issues.title: SEC-6.11 TLDR-detail mismatch: timestamp anchoring claim in TLDR not supported by detail text
issues.description: Section 6.11 TLDR says 'the timestamp infrastructure that supports them anchors the observation log rather than the predictions themselves (see Timestamp Error tab)' but the detail text has no mention of timestamps, OTS, or the wrong-file issue. Per curmudgeon prompt, a TLDR that contradicts or unsupported by the evidence/verdict text is major severity.
